purpose: compact end-to-end proof for preflight, remote snapshot hash listing, dry-run plan upload, batched apply, journal inspect, and inspect-first recovery with explicit pull provenance mapping

pull_pipeline.exporter: scans the merge base and coverage evidence
pull_pipeline.importer: persists the base package as immutable provenance

push_phases.preflight: binds the persisted pull base package to one live remote identity and one short-lived push session
push_phases.snapshot_hash_listing: reads the live remote comparison surface for planning only and never becomes write authority
push_phases.dry_run_upload: uploads the canonical plan as an eligibility receipt, not a lock
push_phases.batch_apply: revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and at the storage boundary
push_phases.journal_inspect: reads durable claim, lease, and fencing evidence without authorizing mutation
push_phases.recovery_inspect: runs before any mutating recovery branch and classifies finish, rollback, retry, or block
push_phases.recovery_mutate: may mutate only after inspect proves the branch safe with fresh live evidence and the same auth floor as the write path

pull_to_push_mapping.exporter: scans the merge base and coverage evidence
pull_to_push_mapping.importer: persists the base package as immutable provenance
pull_to_push_mapping.preflight: binds the persisted pull base to the live remote identity and a short-lived push session
pull_to_push_mapping.push_snapshot_hashes: performs the remote snapshot hash listing for planning only and never becomes write authority
pull_to_push_mapping.push_plan_dry_run: uploads the canonical dry-run plan as eligibility evidence and returns a receipt, not a lock
pull_to_push_mapping.push_batch_apply: revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and again at the storage boundary
pull_to_push_mapping.push_journal: reads durable evidence without authorizing mutation
pull_to_push_mapping.push_recover inspect: starts with inspect and classifies finish, rollback, retry, or block before any mutating repair
pull_to_push_mapping.push_recover auto|finish|rollback: mutates only when the journal plus fresh live hashes prove the action

push_guards.snapshot_hash_listing: returns live remote comparison evidence for planning only and never upgrades into write authority
push_guards.dry_run_receipt: proves eligibility only and cannot be reused as a liveness lock
push_guards.apply_revalidation: refreshes live evidence before every batch and again at the storage boundary
push_guards.journal_inspect: reads durable claim and fencing evidence without mutating target resources
push_guards.recovery_inspect: must happen before any mutating recovery mode and may block when finish or rollback cannot be proven safe
push_guards.remote_liveness: dry-run and apply are separate remote operations
push_guards.dry_run: eligibility evidence only and never a lock
push_guards.apply: must revalidate the live remote before every batch and at the storage boundary
push_guards.recovery: inspect-first and mutates only when journal evidence plus fresh live hashes prove the action
push_guards.auth_floor: at least as strict as current Reprint HMAC usage

bridge_rule: the importer-owned base package is immutable provenance for planning, apply, journal, and recovery

proof: remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity observed at different times
local-edited carries the imported local edits used to build the canonical plan
runner owns preflight, remote snapshot hash listing, dry-run plan upload, batch apply, journal inspect, and recovery
pull exporter/importer establish the immutable base package before push
dry-run is a receipt, not a lock
apply revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and at the storage boundary
journal inspect is read-only
recovery inspect happens before any mutating repair
Docker and Playground both keep the same remote identity mapping and route names
browser-visible inspection stays on the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy
remote-base seeds the persisted pull base
remote-changed reuses the same remote identity after drift
remote snapshot hash listing stays planning-only
apply revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and at the storage boundary
remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity at different times
push preflight, dry-run, apply, journal, and recovery use the same route names as Docker
browser-visible inspection stays on the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy

remote_changed.role: same remote source site later in time

proof: remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity observed at different times
dry-run and apply remain separate remote calls
apply revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and again at the storage boundary
recovery inspect happens before any mutating repair

required_invariants: dry-run and apply are separate remote operations
remote snapshot hash listing is planning evidence, not write authority
dry-run is a receipt, not a lock
apply must revalidate the live remote before every batch and at the storage boundary
journal inspection is read-only and never authorizes mutation by itself
recovery must begin with inspect before any mutating repair
authentication must be at least as strict as current Reprint HMAC usage
preflight binds the persisted pull base package to one live remote identity and one short-lived push session
pull exporter/importer establish the immutable base package before push
one remote source site, one imported local site, and one drift witness are enough to prove the production topology
